title: zizia aurea f. obtusifolia
intro: Zizia aurea f. obtusifolia, also known as meadow parsley or golden alexanders, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its carminative, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The plant is used to relieve symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, thanks to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles. Zizia aurea f. obtusifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal purposes, and its seeds and roots are often used in traditional herbal preparations.
